      This also appears to be open source.

      1. 2
        lilac 2 days ago

        how long until this post gets tagged as videcoding over that fact?

        1. 8
          WilhelmVonWeiner 2 days ago

          Who cares? If you don't have the vibecoding tag muted, it really couldn't matter less.

          1. 2
            junon edited 2 days ago

            Infinitely long given this is to showcase a real thing (oxide.computer which is a pretty well known thing) via a cool marketing graphic that happens to be built in part with AI. The code isn't what's being showcased here.

            1. 5
              wofo 38 hours ago

              And yet, the vibecoding tag has in fact been applied by now

              1. 5
                zk 46 hours ago

                100% true. But I think GP is implying despite that, the Lobster.rs community is very trigger happy with the vibecoding tag. (To which I would kind of agree).

                1. ~
                  lilac 29 hours ago

                  and I am the kind of person who would like to be able to mute the vibecoding tag, but I might as well delete my account at that point.

                  1. ~
                    zk edited 28 hours ago

                    Yeah same here. I have voiced some alternatives before.
                    I've accepted the tag has lost all meaning now and nothing will change. /shrug

                  2. 5
                    jperkin 36 hours ago

                    It's completely ruining the experience for me, and I'm pretty much done with this site at this point. It's not even correct in its use of the term.

                  3. ~
                    lilac 29 hours ago

                    infinitely long huh?

                    1. ~
                      miro 29 hours ago

                      I honestly don't get this. It's a showcase marketing demo made by an employee so I find it highly unlikely they would slop away with something that's not correct. The project is in good shape with no obvious marks this was vibecoded.

                      Yeah there is CLAUDE.md in the repository but come on now, that's a bit too pedantic if that's the sole reason the tag was added. If they were to publish some of their low-level stuff full of em-dashes and goofy comments I wouldn't bat an eye on the tag, but this is a static promotional website whose sole purpose is to be good looking...

              2. 1
                hwj 41 hours ago

                This doesn't seem to work in LibreWolf...

                1. 1
                  jbe_ 41 hours ago

                  It does, but you will have to click the symbol on the address bar and enable webgl. It is one of their default fingerprint resisting measures.
